_comment: Load-bearing ADR claims, asserted at PR time by scripts/check-adr-claims.mjs. Closes axis 7 of the drift-audit framework (ADR-0057). Each entry names a claim made by an ADR or load-bearing prose doc plus the codebase location where the claim should be visible. Schema: {adr, claim, file, match: 'regex'|'contains'|'exists', pattern, expected (regex only)}. Adding a new claim: append to `claims`. Removing a deprecated ADR: delete its entries. `node scripts/check-adr-claims.mjs --list` prints the inventory.

claims.claim: Invitation rate limit — globalPerMonth default = 1000
Invitation rate limit — perWorkspacePerDay default = 50
Invitation rate limit — perUserPerDay default = 20
EMERGENCY_STOP env flag short-circuits Knowlex /api/kb/ask
EMERGENCY_STOP module exists
Free-tier compliance check script exists
Eval golden_qa.json declares maxP95LatencyMs = 10000
Eval golden_qa.json declares minPassRate = 0.6
Eval retry-fetch helper exists
knowledge prisma schema declares pgvector extension
knowlex a11y gate is wired in ci.yml

apps/knowledge vercel-build runs prisma migrate deploy

apps/knowledge postinstall regenerates attestation data

Server-side broadcast uses the private-board- channel prefix (defence-in-depth, not access-control-by-id-secrecy)
Channel-name helpers are pinned by Vitest (single contract surface for server-emit / client-subscribe / auth-route allow-list)

Schema canary EXPECTED includes Membership table (axis 2 coverage extended)
Auth/access matrix pinned by Vitest (15 cases: read/write × demo/non-demo × authed/anonymous)
Auth.js migration shipped (additive table additions: User/Account/Session/VerificationToken/Membership)
Judge rubric module (prompt + parser + aggregator) exists
Judge rubric Vitest pinning the prompt + parser + aggregator invariants exists
Default judge model is gemini-2.5-pro (stronger than the gemini-2.5-flash generator — invariant pinned by Vitest)
eval.ts honors --judge CLI flag and EVAL_JUDGE env (opt-in, default off per ADR-0046 free-tier compliance)
eval.ts imports the judge-rubric module (proves the wiring is real, not stubbed)
RRF fusion module exists
RRF fusion Vitest pinning the rank-only fusion invariants exists
retrieve.ts honors HYBRID_RETRIEVAL_ENABLED env flag (opt-in, default off for run-to-run eval comparability)
Lexical retrieval uses Postgres FTS via plainto_tsquery + ts_rank_cd (BM25-equivalent in spirit)
Schema canary EXPECTED includes the tsv tsvector column (axis 2 of ADR-0057 covers the migration)
FTS migration shipped (additive tsvector column + GIN index)
ADR-0061 names the CI Credentials provider for Knowlex as an 'intentionally not replicated' future follow-up — the architectural gap this calibration attempt surfaced
Eval client makes unauthenticated POST to /api/kb/ingest — the gap surface that lands the 401 on a post-v0.5.12 server
ADR-0011 § Implementation status carries the v0.5.15-rc.0 calibration-status note pointing at ADR-0064
ADR-0063 § Calibration status carries the v0.5.15-rc.0 honest-disclose note referencing ADR-0064

Knowlex next.config.ts carries the build-time assertion blocking VERCEL=1 + E2E_ENABLED=1
claims.pattern: Knowlex CI-only Credentials provider must never

claims.claim: eval.ts carries the CI session acquisition dance (CSRF + signin + cookie injection)

claims.pattern: Run Knowlex locally with your own API key
